channel.title: 1 Chronicles on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in 1 Chronicles on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: 1 Chronicles 1
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 1

1 Adam father of Seth father of Enosh father of 2 Kenan father of Mahalalel father of Jared father of 3 Enoch father of Methuselah father of Lamech father of 4 Noah father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

5 The sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, Tiras

 6The sons of Gomer were Ashkenaz, Diphath, Togarmah;

item.title: 1 Chronicles 2
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 2

1 The sons of Israel: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun, 2 Dan, Joseph, Benjamin,

            Naphtali, Gad, and Asher

3 The sons of JUDAH: Er, Onan, and Shelah by Bath-shua the Canaanitess

            Er, Judah’s firstborn, was sinful; so the LORD took his life.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 3
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 3

         3:1These were David’s six sons born in Hebron:

                        the firstborn was Ammon by Ahinoam the Jezreelitess;

                        the second was Daniel by Abigail the Carmelitess;

                        2 the third was Absalom son of Maacah daughter of Talmai, king of Geshur;

item.title: 1 Chronicles 4
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 4

1 The sons of JUDAH: Perez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur, and Shobal

         2 Reaiah son of Shobal was father of Jahath father of Ahumai and Lahad. These were the families of the Zorathites.

         3 These were the sons of Etam: Jezreel, Ishma, and Idbash; their sister was Hazzelelponi.

         4 Penuel was father of Gedor and Ezer father of Hushah. Those were the sons of Hur, firstborn of Ephrathah ancestor of Bethlehem.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 5
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 5

5:1, 3The sons of REUBEN, Israel’s firstborn: 3Hanoch and Pallu, Hezron and Carmi

 (Because he defiled his father’s bed, his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph son of Israel; so he’s not enrolled in the genealogy by birthright. 2 Although Judah prevailed over his brothers and the leader came from him, the birthright was Joseph’s.)

item.title: 1 Chronicles 6
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 6

1 The descendants of LEVI: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari

         2 The descendants of Kohath were Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.

                  3 The children of Amram were Aaron, Moses, and Miriam.

                           The sons of Aaron were Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 7
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 7

1 ISSACHAR had four sons: Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron.

            2 The sons of Tola were Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Ibsam, and Samuel, heads of

            families. Tola’s sons were trained soldiers in their generations. At the time

            of David they numbered 22,600.

                  3 The son of Uzzi was Izrahiah.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 8
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 8

1 BENJAMIN was father of Bela his firstborn, Ashbel second, Aharah third, 2 Nohah fourth, and Rapha fifth.

         3 Bela was father of Addar, Gera, Abihud, 4 Abishua, Naaman, Ahoah, 5 Gera, Shephuphan, and Huram.

         6 These are the sons of Ehud, heads of families among the people in Geba. They were exiled to Manahath: 7 Naaman, Ahijah, and Gera, father of Uzza and Ahihud.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 9
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 9

Those were all descendants of Benjamin.

1 Ch 8:29-40

1 So Israel was enrolled by genealogies. They’re recorded in The Book of the Kings of Israel. Judah was exiled to Babylon because they were unfaithful.

2 The first to live again in their towns were Israel, the priests, the Levites, and the Temple servants. 3 Some descendants of Judah, Benjamin, Ephraim, and Manasseh lived in Jerusalem:

item.title: 1 Chronicles 10
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 10

The Death of Saul and His Sons

1 The Philistines attacked Israel, and they fled and fell dead on Mount Gilboa. 2 The Philistines overtook Saul and his sons and struck down his sons Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malchi-shua. 3 The battle grew intense against Saul, and the archers wounded him. 4 He told his armor bearer, “Draw your sword and thrust me through so these uncircumcised Philistines don’t come and torture me.” But his armor bearer was too afraid to do it; so Saul took his sword and fell on it. 5 When his armor bearer saw he was dead, he fell on his sword too. 6 So Saul’s whole dynasty died together.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 11
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 11

David Anointed King

1 Then Israel gathered to David in Hebron,

“We’re your flesh and bone. 2 When Saul was king, you led out and brought in Israel. The LORD your God said you would shepherd his people and be their leader.”

3 The elders in Israel came to him, and he made a treaty with them in the LORD’s presence. They anointed him king according to the LORD’s message through Samuel.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 12
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 12

Warriors Join David

1 These are men that came over to David at Ziklag, while he still couldn’t move around freely because of Saul. They were among the elite soldiers that helped him during war. 2 They used bows. With either hand they could sling stones and shoot arrows. They were Saul’s relatives from Benjamin. 3 The chief was Ahiezer, then Joash, sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite; Jeziel and Pelet, sons of Azmaveth; Beracah and Jehu the Anathothite; 4 Ishmaiah the Gibeonite, an elite soldier among the 30 and a leader over them. Then came Jeremiah, Jahaziel, Johanan, Jozabad the Gederathite, 5 Eluzai, Jerimoth, Bealiah, Shemariah, Shephatiah the Haruphite; 6 Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, Jashobeam, the Korahites; 7 Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jerobam of Gedor.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 13
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 13

The Ark Brought from Kiriath-Jearim

1 Then David consulted with every commander of thousands and hundreds, 2 and told the whole assembly,

“If it seems good to you and if the LORD our God wills, let’s send around to our relatives that are still in Israel, as well as to the priests and Levites that are with them in their towns and pastures, to have them meet with us. 3 Let’s bring the Ark back. We neglected it in Saul’s time.”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 14
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 14

David’s Wives and Children

1 Hiram, king of Tyre, sent ambassadors to David with cedar trees, masons, and carpenters, to build him a palace. 2 David saw that the LORD had made him secure as king over Israel, and that his kingdom was highly regarded for Israel’s sake. 3 He took more wives at Jerusalem and had more sons and daughters. 4 His children born in Jerusalem were Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon, 5 Ibhar, Elishua, Elpelet, 6 Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia, 7 Elishama, Beeliada, and Eliphelet. 1 Ch 14:1-7

item.title: 1 Chronicles 15
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 15

The Ark Brought to Jerusalem

1 David built houses for himself in The City of David and prepared a place for the Ark of God and pitched a tent for it. 2 He said, “Don’t anybody carry the Ark but Levites. The LORD chose them to carry it and serve him permanently.” 3 He gathered Israel to Jerusalem to bring the Ark up to the place he’d prepared. 4 He gathered the descendants of Aaron and the Levites:

item.title: 1 Chronicles 16
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 16

1 They put the Ark in the tent David had pitched for it and offered burnt offerings and peace offerings to God. 2 When David had finished offering them, he blessed the people in the LORD’s name. 3 To every man and woman in Israel, he passed out a loaf of bread, a helping of meat, and a raisin cake.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 17
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 17

The LORD’s Covenant with David

1 While David was living in his palace, he told Nathan the prophet, “I’m living in a cedar palace, but the LORD’s Ark is under curtains.”

2 Nathan said, “Do what’s in your mind. God is with you.”                                    

3 That same night, the message from God came to him,

item.title: 1 Chronicles 18
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 18

David’s Military Victories

1 After that, David defeated the Philistines and took Gath and its surrounding towns. 2 He defeated Moab and they brought tribute. 3 He defeated Hadadezer, king of Zobah, as far as Hamath and secured his rule all the way to the Euphrates River. 4 He took 1,000 chariots, 7,000 cavalry, and 20,000 foot soldiers. He hamstrung the chariot horses but reserved enough for 100 chariots.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 19
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 19

David Defeats the Ammonites and Syrians

1 After this, Nahash, king of the Ammonites, died and his son became king. 2 David said, “I’ll be kind to Hanun because his father was kind to me.” He sent ambassadors to console him about his father. 3 But the officials in Ammon told Hanun, “Do you think David is honoring your father by sending ambassadors to comfort you? Haven’t they come to spy out the land to overthrow it?” 4 Hanun took David’s ambassadors and shaved them, cut off their cloaks as high as their hips, and sent them away. 5 Some people came and told David about the men. He sent to meet them because they were humiliated. He told them to stay in Jericho till their beards grew back; then come home.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 20
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 20

The Capture of Rabbah

1 In the spring, when kings go to war, Joab led out the army and ravaged the country of the Ammonites and laid siege to Rabbah. David stayed in Jerusalem. Joab attacked Rabbah and overthrew it. 2 Then David went to Rabbah and took the crown off their king’s head, and found that it weighed 75½ lbs a talent of gold, and was set with precious stones. They placed it on David’s head, and he brought out a really large amount of spoil from the city. 3 He brought the people out, and cut them with saws, sharp instruments, and axes. He did that to all the Ammonite towns. Then he and the people came back to Jerusalem.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 21
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 21

David’s Ill-Advised Census

1 Satan stood up against Israel and tempted David to take a census. 2 David told Joab and the commanders of the troops,

“Go count Israel from Dan to Beersheba and bring me the results so I can know how many there are.”

 3 Joab said, “May the LORD add to his people 100 times as many as they are! But my lord the king, aren’t they all serving you? Why do you want to know this? Why bring guilt on Israel?”

item.title: 1 Chronicles 22
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 22

David’s Preparations for the Temple

2 David issued an order to gather the foreigners in Israel and set stonecutters to dress stones for God’s Temple. 3 He provided large stores of iron for making nails for the doors in the gates and for clamps, more bronze than could be weighed, 4 and cedar logs without number. The Sidonians and Tyrians brought him large supplies of cedar timber. 5 He said,

item.title: 1 Chronicles 23
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 23

Duties of Levites

1 When David got old, he appointed Solomon king over Israel. 2 He gathered the leaders with the priests and Levites. 3 The Levites 30 years old and up numbered 38,000. 4 Of these, 24,000 oversaw the work, 6,000 were officers and judges, 5 4,000 were gatekeepers, and 4,000 praised the LORD with instruments David made for that purpose. 6 He divided them according to the sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.     1 Ch 23:1-6

item.title: 1 Chronicles 24
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 24

Duties of Priests

1 These were the divisions of Aaron’s descendants. His sons were Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar. 2 But Nadab and Abihu died before their father did, and they didn’t have any sons; so Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests. 3 David—with Zadok from Eleazar’s descendants and Ahimelech from Ithamar’s descendants—divided them by their offices for their service. 4 Since there were more chief men descended from Eleazar than from Ithamar, they divided them into 16 heads descended from Eleazar and 8 from Ithamar. 5 They divided them alike by lot because they were officers of the sanctuary and officers of God. 6 Shemaiah son of Nethanel the secretary from the Levites, recorded them in the presence of the king, the officials, Zadok the priest, Ahimelech son of Abiathar, and the heads of the families among the priests and Levites—one family taken for Eleazar and one for Ithamar.                                                                                                           1 Ch 24:1-6

item.title: 1 Chronicles 25
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 25

Duties of Musicians

1 David and the commanders of the army set apart some from sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun to prophesy with lyres, harps, and cymbals. Those who performed their service were as follows:

2 from the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asharelah were under the direction of Asaph, who prophesied under the direction of the king;

item.title: 1 Chronicles 26
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 26

Duties of Gatekeepers

1 For the divisions of the gatekeepers, from the Korahites there was Meshelemiah son of Kore from the sons of Asaph. 2 Meshelemiah/Shelemiah had sons: Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael second, Zebadiah third, Jathniel fourth, 3 Elam fifth, Johanan sixth, Eliehoenai seventh.

4 Obed-edom had sons: Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad second, Joah third, Sacar fourth, Nethanel fifth, 5 Ammiel sixth, Issachar seventh, and Peullethai eighth. God had blessed him. 6 Shemaiah had sons who governed their family because they were brave soldiers: 7 Othni, Rephael, Obed, and Elzabad, whose brothers Elihu and Semachiah were brave men. 8 Obed-edom’s sons, their sons and relatives were capable and strong for the service—62.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 27
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 27

Military Divisions

1 These were the heads of families, commanders of thousands and hundreds, and their officers that served the king in regard to the divisions that came in and went out month by month all year. Each division numbered 24,000. 

2 Jashobeam son of Zabdiel was responsible for the first division for the first month. 3 He descended from Perez and was chief of the commanders of the army for the first month.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 28
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 28

1 David gathered to Jerusalem the officials in Israel, the leaders of the tribes, the commanders of the divisions that served the king, the commanders over thousands and hundreds, the overseers of the property and livestock that belong to the king and his sons, the officials and elite soldiers, all the brave warriors. 2 He stood up and said,

“My relatives, my people, I intended to build a permanent temple for the Ark of the Covenant and for a footstool of our God; so I made preparations. 3 But he told me, ‘You won’t build me a house; you’re a soldier that’s shed blood.’ 4 But the LORD chose me from my father’s family to rule Israel permanently. He chose Judah to lead, chose my father’s family in Judah, and delighted in me among my brothers. 5 From my sons he’s chosen Solomon to sit on the throne. (The LORD has given me many sons.) 6 He told me, ‘Solomon will build my temple and courts. I’ve chosen him to be my son, and I’ll be his father. 7 I’ll make his kingdom permanently secure if he’s resolute in keeping my commandments and ordinances, like he is today.’ 8 In front of Israel, in the assembly of the LORD, and in the hearing of our God, endeavor to follow all his commandments so you can keep the good land and leave it for your descendants.

item.title: 1 Chronicles 29
item.description:
1 Chronicles Chapter 29

Offerings for the Temple

1 David told the assembly,

“God alone has chosen Solomon.  He’s young and new to the work. It’s an important undertaking; the Temple won’t be for some mortal but for the LORD God. 2 I’ve provided as much material as I could: gold, silver, bronze, iron, and wood—plus large amounts of onyx, gems for settings, antimony, colored stones, marble—all kinds of precious stones. 3 In addition, I’m giving my own gold and silver: 4 113¼ tons 3,000 talents of gold from Ophir, 264¼ tons 7,000 talents of refined silver for overlaying the walls of the Temple, 5 and the gold and silver items the craftsmen will make. Who will offer willingly, consecrating himself to the LORD today?”
